title: Equations with Variables on Both Sides

epigraph.text: Come now, let us reason together, says the Lord.

content.text:
Some equations have variables on both sides of the equals sign. To 
solve these, we first gather all variable terms on one side and all 
constant terms on the other.

When variables appear on both sides, we need to "gather" them to 
one side, just as a shepherd gathers sheep.

content.title: Variables on Both Sides
content.content:
1. Use addition or subtraction to move all variable terms to one side

2. Use addition or subtraction to move all constant terms to the 
 other side

3. Solve the resulting one- or two-step equation

4. Check your answer

content.title: Basic example
content.solution:
$$
5x + 3 &= 2x + 15 && \text{Original equation} \\
5x + 3 - 2x &= 2x + 15 - 2x && \text{Subtract __MATH_BLOCK_0__ from both sides} \\
3x + 3 &= 15 && \text{Simplify} \\
3x + 3 - 3 &= 15 - 3 && \text{Subtract 3 from both sides} \\
3x &= 12 \\
x &= 4 && \text{Divide both sides by 3}
$$

**Check:** 

- Left side: $5(4) + 3 = 20 + 3 = 23$

- Right side: $2(4) + 15 = 8 + 15 = 23$

content.title: Variables with subtraction
content.solution:
$$
7y - 4 &= 3y + 8 \\
7y - 4 - 3y &= 3y + 8 - 3y && \text{Subtract __MATH_BLOCK_0__ from both sides} \\
4y - 4 &= 8 \\
4y - 4 + 4 &= 8 + 4 && \text{Add 4 to both sides} \\
4y &= 12 \\
y &= 3 && \text{Divide both sides by 4}
$$

**Check:** Left: $7(3) - 4 = 17$. Right: $3(3) + 8 = 17$

content.text: Sometimes the solution will be negative. This is perfectly valid!

content.title: Negative solution
content.solution:
$$
2x + 10 &= 5x + 1 \\
2x + 10 - 5x &= 5x + 1 - 5x && \text{Subtract __MATH_BLOCK_0__ from both sides} \\
-3x + 10 &= 1 \\
-3x + 10 - 10 &= 1 - 10 && \text{Subtract 10 from both sides} \\
-3x &= -9 \\
x &= 3 && \text{Divide both sides by __MATH_BLOCK_1__}
$$

**Check:** Left: $2(3) + 10 = 16$. Right: $5(3) + 1 = 16$

content.content:
Whether answers are positive or negative, large or small, the process 
of solving remains the same. God's truth operates the same way—it 
applies universally, regardless of our circumstances.

content.text: Sometimes you must simplify each side before gathering variables.

content.title: Distribution required
content.solution:
$$
3(x + 2) &= x + 10 \\
3x + 6 &= x + 10 && \text{Distribute the 3} \\
3x + 6 - x &= x + 10 - x && \text{Subtract __MATH_BLOCK_0__ from both sides} \\
2x + 6 &= 10 \\
2x &= 4 && \text{Subtract 6 from both sides} \\
x &= 2 && \text{Divide both sides by 2}
$$

**Check:** Left: $3(2 + 2) = 3(4) = 12$. Right: $2 + 10 = 12$

content.title: Distribution on both sides
content.solution:
$$
2(x + 4) &= 3(x - 1) \\
2x + 8 &= 3x - 3 && \text{Distribute on both sides} \\
2x + 8 - 3x &= 3x - 3 - 3x && \text{Subtract __MATH_BLOCK_0__ from both sides} \\
-x + 8 &= -3 \\
-x &= -11 && \text{Subtract 8 from both sides} \\
x &= 11 && \text{Multiply both sides by __MATH_BLOCK_1__}
$$

**Check:** Left: $2(11 + 4) = 2(15) = 30$. Right: $3(11 - 1) = 3(10) = 30$

content.title: Identity (infinite solutions)
content.solution:
$$
2(x + 3) &= 2x + 6 \\
2x + 6 &= 2x + 6 && \text{Distribute} \\
2x + 6 - 2x &= 2x + 6 - 2x \\
6 &= 6 && \text{Always true!}
$$

This is an **identity**—it's true for all values of $x$. 
The solution is **all real numbers**.

content.title: Contradiction (no solution)
content.solution:
$$
3x + 5 &= 3x + 8 \\
3x + 5 - 3x &= 3x + 8 - 3x \\
5 &= 8 && \text{Never true!}
$$

This is a **contradiction**—there is **no solution**.

marginNotes.text:
Whoever is not with me is against me, 
and whoever does not gather with me scatters.

And we know that in all things God works 
for the good of those who love him.

Call to me and I will answer you and 
tell you great and unsearchable things you do not know.

marginNotes.author: Euclid
marginNotes.text: There is no royal road to geometry.

marginNotes.author: Isaac Newton
marginNotes.text:
If I have seen further, it is by standing 
on the shoulders of giants.
